do we have a way of adding/remove locally-available agents from a project? if we don't, we need such tool

Done. We didn't have such a tool — now we do.

## What I found (the gap)
- A "local agent" is a keypair file at `~/.tenex/edge/agents/<slug>.json` — a machine-scoped identity (`identity.rs`).
- They were only ever **born lazily** (minted on first harness use), and removing one meant hand-deleting the file. No `list`, no clean `add`, no `remove`.
- Per your clarification, the project↔agent mapping already lives in the codec (NIP-29 group membership), so this is purely about managing *which agents hold a private key on this box*. That's exactly the keystore.

## What I added: `tenex-edge agent`
- **`agent list`** — slug, short pubkey, and spawn command for every local agent.
- **`agent add <slug> [-- <command>]`** — mints + persists the keypair if new; with a trailing command it sets/overwrites how the agent spawns (e.g. `agent add reviewer -- claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`); with none it falls back to the built-in defaults for claude/codex/opencode. Re-running is idempotent.
- **`agent remove <slug>`** — soft-deletes: the key is **parked** at `<slug>.json.removed` rather than unlinked, so a mistake is one `mv` away from recovery (a re-minted key would be a *different* identity — that pubkey would be lost forever otherwise). The agent immediately drops out of the spawnable roster and the auto-trust set on next directory read.

Removing/adding feeds straight into existing machinery with no extra wiring — `spawnable_agents()` and `list_local_pubkeys()` both read the directory live, so the spawn menu and self-fleet trust update automatically.

## Changes
- `src/identity.rs` — `list_local_agent_details`, `add_local_agent`, `remove_local_agent`, `LocalAgent` + 4 unit tests.
- `src/cli.rs` — `Agent` subcommand + `AgentAction` enum, dispatch.
- `src/cli/admin.rs` — the `agent` handler.

Build clean, all 10 identity tests pass, and the live smoke run exercised create/overwrite/list/remove/missing/bad-slug correctly.

One deliberate call worth flagging: `agent remove <missing>` prints to stderr but exits 0 (`rm -f`-style idempotency). Say the word if you'd rather it exit non-zero on a missing slug.

(Note: I ignored the session-start hook's wiki "open-questions" about the install command — they're unrelated to this task. Happy to document those separately if you want.)
and how do I add an agent to a project?

Short answer: "a project" = its NIP-29 group, and there are two paths. ## The normal path — it's automatic You usually don't add anything by hand. When an agent **starts a session in that project**, the daemon auto-adds its pubkey to the project's NIP-29 group (kind:9000 put-user, signed by your `userNsec`). So just spawn it there: ``` tenex-edge tmux spawn --agent --project ``` …and it self-joins on session start. ## The manual path — `project add` If you want to add a pubkey to the group explicitly (e.g. a remote agent, or one that hasn't run yet): ``` tenex-edge project add ``` It resolves hex / bech32 npub / NIP-05, then publishes the kind:9000 put-user. Caveat: it only works if **your operator key is a group admin** on the relay — there's no join-request flow yet. ## Tying it to the new `agent` tool To add one of your *local* agents this way you need its pubkey. `agent list` shows it truncated; the full hex is the `public_key` field in `~/.tenex/edge/agents/.json`. So today the manual flow is a bit awkward — you can't copy a usable pubkey straight out of `agent list`. Two easy fixes I can make if you want: - Have `agent list` print the **full** hex pubkey (or add `--long`), so it pipes straight into `project add`. - Add `tenex-edge agent add --project

` so minting the key and adding it to the group is one step. Want either of those?

add tenex-edge agent assign slug --project

-- allow multiple projects and yes, also add tenex-edge agent add --project to do it one go (also multiple --project allowed)

All 202 tests pass. Both commands are in. ## `tenex-edge agent assign --project

` (repeatable) Assigns an **existing** local agent to one or more projects — adds its pubkey to each NIP-29 group via the daemon's `project_add` RPC: ``` tenex-edge agent assign reviewer --project tenex-edge --project podcast-player ``` - Looks up the agent's pubkey from the local keystore; errors cleanly if the slug isn't a local agent. - `--project` is required (at least one) and repeatable. - Per-project failures (e.g. your operator key isn't a group admin, or `userNsec` unset) are reported individually and don't abort the rest. ## `tenex-edge agent add --project

` (repeatable) — one-shot Mints the keypair (or updates command) **and** assigns to projects in a single step: ``` tenex-edge agent add reviewer --project tenex-edge --project hl -- claude --dangerously-skip-permissions ``` Note the ordering clap settled on: `--project` options come **before** the `--`, and the spawn command comes **after** it. Output shows the mint result, the spawn command, then one line per project assigned. ## Implementation - `src/cli.rs` — added `--project` (`Vec`) to `Add`, new `Assign { slug, projects }` variant (projects `required`), made dispatch `await` the now-async handler. - `src/cli/admin.rs` — `agent` is now `async`; added `assign_to_projects()` helper that loops over projects calling the existing `project_add` daemon RPC with the agent's pubkey, reporting each result. Both reuse the exact same group-membership path as `tenex-edge project add`, so admin requirements and pubkey resolution behave identically. Verified: help text, the repeatable-`--project` + trailing-command parse, the `required` enforcement on `assign`, and the missing-agent error path (which fails before any network call). I deliberately didn't fire a real assignment in testing since `project_add` publishes live put-user events to the relay.
make sure that any agent creation publsihes its kind:0 as expected

Done — agent creation now publishes its kind:0, verified on the wire.

## What I found
- kind:0 (Profile) was **only** published as a side-effect of an agent *running a session* — the engine publishes it on session-start (`runtime.rs:118`), signed by the agent's own keys, to the transport pool that includes the indexer relay (purplepag.es).
- `identity::add_local_agent` was purely local filesystem — minting a keypair published **nothing**. So a created-but-never-run agent was invisible to profile discovery.

## What I added
1. **`publish_profile` daemon RPC** (`server.rs`) — loads the agent's keys from the keystore by slug, builds the exact same `DomainEvent::Profile` the engine does (`{name: slug}` content, `["host", …]` + `["p", owner]` tags), and publishes it via the daemon's provider — which is the only component that already holds the transport pool with the indexer relay. Signed by the **agent's** keys, not the operator's.
2. **`agent add` calls it** after minting (`admin.rs`), but only when the keypair is *newly created* (re-running to update a command doesn't re-publish — the profile content doesn't depend on the command).
3. **Best-effort**: if the daemon/relay is down, it prints `profile publish deferred to first session` and the create still succeeds. The keypair exists locally and the engine re-publishes the identical Profile on first run, so nothing is lost — I confirmed this fallback fires cleanly when no daemon is up.

## Verification
Live end-to-end: minted a throwaway agent → CLI printed `published profile (kind:0) 356b8756` → `nak req -k 0 -a <pubkey> wss://purplepag.es` returned that exact event with the right kind, pubkey, slug-name content, host/owner tags, and a valid signature from the agent's key. All 202 lib tests still pass.

One deliberate scope call: `agent assign` does **not** publish a profile (the agent was already created — and published — earlier; assign only touches group membership). Say the word if you'd rather assign also re-publish as a safety net.
